excluded_defs.name: MarkFlagsMutuallyExclusive
excluded_defs.reason: read to understand how annotations are set; already calls mergePersistentFlags so not itself the bug site

excluded_defs.name: hasAllFlags
excluded_defs.reason: read to understand why persistent flags might be silently skipped; hasAllFlags returns false when flag is absent from the flag set, which is the root cause

exploration_log.aha_moment: hasAllFlags silently skips the whole group when a persistent flag is not yet in c.Flags() — calling mergePersistentFlags before getting c.Flags() ensures the complete flag set is present.
exploration_log.hindsight: Should have immediately added mergePersistentFlags at the top of ValidateFlagGroups; the pattern is used in all other flag-inspection methods.

key_decisions.reasoning: Both functions use c.Flags() without merging persistent flags first; the same issue affects completion enforcement

solve_notes: Added `c.mergePersistentFlags()` at the start of both `ValidateFlagGroups` and `enforceFlagGroupsForCompletion`. Without this call, if `ValidateFlagGroups` is invoked in a code path that hasn't already merged persistent flags, `c.Flags()` returns only the local flag set, causing `hasAllFlags` to return false for inherited persistent flags and silently skipping the entire mutual-exclusion group.
task_text:
Fix MarkFlagsMutuallyExclusive not checking persistent flags from parent commands

`MarkFlagsMutuallyExclusive("format", "json")` only checks flags defined
on the current command, not persistent flags inherited from a parent
command. When one flag is local and the other is persistent, the
mutual exclusion check is silently skipped. Fix the flag group validation
in `flag_groups.go` to resolve flags from the full flag set including
persistent flags.

thrash_preventing_defs.name: mergePersistentFlags
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: understanding what mergePersistentFlags does (adds parent persistent flags to c.flags) is essential to knowing why adding it fixes the persistent-flag lookup

thrash_preventing_defs.name: hasAllFlags
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: this helper returns false when a flag is absent from the flag set, which is how groups are silently skipped — understanding it confirms why merging flags fixes the problem

tier_difference_reasoning: minimum_sufficient_defs contains the two edited methods. thrash_preventing adds mergePersistentFlags (so the agent understands what the fix does and why it works) and hasAllFlags (so the agent understands the silent-skip mechanism and can verify the fix addresses the root cause without backtracking).
